devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । निशम्य वाचं वदतो मुनेः पुण्यतमां नृप । भूयः पप्रच्छ कौरव्यो वासुदेवकथादृतः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaniśamya vācaṃ vadato muneḥ puṇyatamāṃ nṛpabhūyaḥ papraccha kauravyo vāsudevakathādṛtaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Oh king, hearing the holiest discourse of the sage, Vidura (a descendant of Kuru) who cherished high respects for narratives of Hari, again asked (Maitreya).

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । स वै स्वायम्भुवः सम्राट् प्रियः पुत्रः स्वयम्भुवः । प्रतिलभ्य प्रियां पत्‍नीं किं चकार ततो मुने ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvācasa vai svāyambhuvaḥ samrāṭ priyaḥ putraḥ svayambhuvaḥpratilabhya priyāṃ pat‍nīṃ kiṃ cakāra tato mune
translation:
Vidura said:
The emperor Svāyambhuva was verily the favourite son of god Brahmā. Oh sage, what did he do after obtaining a loving wife?

devanagari: चरितं तस्य राजर्षेः आदिराजस्य सत्तम । ब्रूहि मे श्रद्दधानाय विष्वक्सेनाश्रयो ह्यसौ ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: caritaṃ tasya rājarṣeḥ ādirājasya sattamabrūhi me śraddadhānāya viṣvaksenāśrayo hyasau
translation:
Oh foremost among the good, narrate to me, who am full of faith, the life of the king-sage, the first ruler of the world, because he also resorted to Vāsudeva (Viṣvaksena).
4[1]. (The act of) listening to the virtuous deeds of those who cherish in their hearts the lotus-like feet of Mukunda, is eulogised by the wise, as the main fruit (result) of men’s study of the śāstras with long efforts.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Subodhinī Even though the virtuous deeds of the Lord are heard with great exertions, they are not properly fixed in the heart and hence do not bear fruit. They are properly fixed by listening to the deeds of those who are his devotees. Hence the propriety of listening to Manu's life.

devanagari: श्रुतस्य पुंसां सुचिरश्रमस्य । नन्वञ्जसा सूरिभिरीडितोऽर्थः । तत्तद्‍गुणानुश्रवणं मुकुन्द । पादारविन्दं हृदयेषु येषाम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutasya puṃsāṃ suciraśramasyananvañjasā sūribhirīḍito'rthaḥtattad‍guṇānuśravaṇaṃ mukundapādāravindaṃ hṛdayeṣu yeṣām

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति ब्रुवाणं विदुरं विनीतं । सहस्रशीर्ष्णश्चरणोपधानम् । प्रहृष्टरोमा भगवत्कथायां । प्रणीयमानो मुनिरभ्यचष्ट ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaiti bruvāṇaṃ viduraṃ vinītaṃsahasraśīrṣṇaścaraṇopadhānamprahṛṣṭaromā bhagavatkathāyāṃpraṇīyamāno munirabhyacaṣṭa
translation: The sage (Maitreya) who was thus urged to narrate the episodes of the Lord had his hair standing on ends (due to intense devotion), and narrated to Vidura who was modest and was (as if) a pillow to the feet of Śrī Kṛṣṇa[2] (lit. the thousand-headed god) requested him (Maitreya) thus.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
As Śrī Kṛṣṇa used to rest his feet on Vidura’s laps—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । यदा स्वभार्यया सार्धं जातः स्वायम्भुवो मनुः । प्राञ्जलिः प्रणतश्चेदं वेदगर्भमभाषत ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācayadā svabhāryayā sārdhaṃ jātaḥ svāyambhuvo manuḥprāñjaliḥ praṇataścedaṃ vedagarbhamabhāṣata
translation:
Maitreya said:
When Svāyambhuva Manu was born along with his wife, he folded his hands and bowing to Brahmā (Vedagarbha) he said:

devanagari: त्वमेकः सर्वभूतानां जन्मकृत् वृत्तिदः पिता । तथापि नः प्रजानां ते शुश्रूषा केन वा भवेत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tvamekaḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ janmakṛt vṛttidaḥ pitātathāpi naḥ prajānāṃ te śuśrūṣā kena vā bhavet
translation: “You are the sole progenitor and nourisher and protector of all beings. But how (in what way) are we, your creatures, to render you service?

devanagari: तद्विधेहि नमस्तुभ्यं कर्मस्वीड्यात्मशक्तिषु । यत्कृत्वेह यशो विष्वक् अमुत्र च भवेद्‍गतिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tadvidhehi namastubhyaṃ karmasvīḍyātmaśaktiṣuyatkṛtveha yaśo viṣvak amutra ca bhaved‍gatiḥ
translation: Oh praiseworthy one, I bow to you. Tell me what action, out of those within our capacity, I should do whereby my fame will spread everywhere (here) and lead to Heaven hereafter.”

devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच । प्रीतस्तुभ्यमहं तात स्वस्ति स्ताद्वां क्षितीश्वर । यन्निर्व्यलीकेन हृदा शाधि मेत्यात्मनार्पितम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmovācaprītastubhyamahaṃ tāta svasti stādvāṃ kṣitīśvarayannirvyalīkena hṛdā śādhi metyātmanārpitam
translation:
Brahmā said:
“Oh child, I am pleased with you. Oh king, blessed be you both. You have offered yourself to me with a guileless (sincere) heart for guidance.

devanagari: एतावत्यात्मजैर्वीर कार्या ह्यपचितिर्गुरौ । शक्त्याप्रमत्तैर्गृह्येत सादरं गतमत्सरैः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: etāvatyātmajairvīra kāryā hyapacitirgurauśaktyāpramattairgṛhyeta sādaraṃ gatamatsaraiḥ
translation: Oh warrior, this much worship should be rendered by children to their father that they should abide by his order respectfully, to the best of their capacity without jealousy or negligence.

devanagari: स त्वमस्यामपत्यानि सदृशान्यात्मनो गुणैः । उत्पाद्य शास धर्मेण गां यज्ञैः पुरुषं यज ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvamasyāmapatyāni sadṛśānyātmano guṇaiḥutpādya śāsa dharmeṇa gāṃ yajñaiḥ puruṣaṃ yaja
translation: Have from her children who are like you in qualities. Protect the earth according to law. Worship the Puruṣa (the Supreme Man) with sacrifices.

devanagari: परं शुश्रूषणं मह्यं स्यात्प्रजारक्षया नृप । भगवांस्ते प्रजाभर्तुः हृषीकेशोऽनुतुष्यति ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: paraṃ śuśrūṣaṇaṃ mahyaṃ syātprajārakṣayā nṛpabhagavāṃste prajābhartuḥ hṛṣīkeśo'nutuṣyati
translation: Oh king, the highest service (rendered) to me will be the protection of the subjects. Lord Hṛṣīkeśa (Viṣṇu) will be ever pleased with you for your being the protector of the subjects.

devanagari: येषां न तुष्टो भगवान् यज्ञलिङ्गो जनार्दनः । तेषां श्रमो ह्यपार्थाय यदात्मा नादृतः स्वयम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yeṣāṃ na tuṣṭo bhagavān yajñaliṅgo janārdanaḥteṣāṃ śramo hyapārthāya yadātmā nādṛtaḥ svayam
translation: Fruitless are the efforts of those with whom Lord Kṛṣṇa (Janārdana) whose form is sacrifice, is not pleased; for, thereby, the Soul (ātman) itself is disrespected.”

devanagari: मनुरुवाच । आदेशेऽहं भगवतो वर्तेयामीवसूदन । स्थानं त्विहानुजानीहि प्रजानां मम च प्रभो ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: manuruvācaādeśe'haṃ bhagavato varteyāmīvasūdanasthānaṃ tvihānujānīhi prajānāṃ mama ca prabho
translation:
Manu said:
“Oh destroyer of sins, I shall abide by the order of your lordship. But fix up a place for me and for my progeny here.

devanagari: यदोकः सर्वसत्त्वानां मही मग्ना महाम्भसि । अस्या उद्धरणे यत्‍नो देव देव्या विधीयताम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yadokaḥ sarvasattvānāṃ mahī magnā mahāmbhasiasyā uddharaṇe yat‍no deva devyā vidhīyatām
translation: The earth which is the place for all beings is submerged in the great ocean. Oh god, make some efforts to lift up this goddess (earth) (from the ocean)”[3].
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Kramasandarbha. This indicates that this is the introduction to the Varāha incarnation at the beginning of the present Kalpa.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । परमेष्ठी त्वपां मध्ये तथा सन्नामवेक्ष्य गाम् । कथमेनां समुन्नेष्य इति दध्यौ धिया चिरम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaparameṣṭhī tvapāṃ madhye tathā sannāmavekṣya gāmkathamenāṃ samunneṣya iti dadhyau dhiyā ciram
translation:
Maitreya said:
Seeing that the earth is submerged that way in waters, god Brahmā pondered over for a long time, “How can I lift her (the earth) up?[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains: At the beginning of creation Brahmā drank up the ocean and restored the earth to its place. He did not understand how suddenly it was immersed in water again.

devanagari: सृजतो मे क्षितिर्वार्भिः प्लाव्यमाना रसां गता । अथात्र किमनुष्ठेयं अस्माभिः सर्गयोजितैः । यस्याहं हृदयादासं स ईशो विदधातु मे ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sṛjato me kṣitirvārbhiḥ plāvyamānā rasāṃ gatāathātra kimanuṣṭheyaṃ asmābhiḥ sargayojitaiḥyasyāhaṃ hṛdayādāsaṃ sa īśo vidadhātu me
translation: While I was engaged in creation, the earth was engulfed by waters and it sank down to Rasātala. What can we, who are entrusted with the duty of creation, do now here? Let the Lord from whose heart I came into existence, do it for me.”

devanagari: इत्यभिध्यायतो नासा विवरात्सहसानघ । वराहतोको निरगाद् अङ्गुष्ठपरिमाणकः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ityabhidhyāyato nāsā vivarātsahasānaghavarāhatoko niragād aṅguṣṭhaparimāṇakaḥ
translation: Oh sinless one, while he was reflecting thus, a thumbsize small boar suddenly dropped down from his nostril.

devanagari: तस्याभिपश्यतः खस्थः क्षणेन किल भारत । गजमात्रः प्रववृधे तदद्‍भुतं अभून्महत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyābhipaśyataḥ khasthaḥ kṣaṇena kila bhāratagajamātraḥ pravavṛdhe tadad‍bhutaṃ abhūnmahat
translation: Oh Vidura (of Bharata clan), while he (Brahmā) was looking on, a great miracle took place: The small boar in the sky shot up to the size of an elephant in a moment.

devanagari: मरीचिप्रमुखैर्विप्रैः कुमारैर्मनुना सह । दृष्ट्वा तत्सौकरं रूपं तर्कयामास चित्रधा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: marīcipramukhairvipraiḥ kumārairmanunā sahadṛṣṭvā tatsaukaraṃ rūpaṃ tarkayāmāsa citradhā
translation: Along with Brāhmaṇas with Marīci as their chief, with Kumāras (Sanaka, Sanandana etc.) and with Manu, he saw the boar form and began to think in various ways.

devanagari: किमेतत्सौकरव्याजं सत्त्वं दिव्यमवस्थितम् । अहो बताश्चर्यमिदं नासाया मे विनिःसृतम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: kimetatsaukaravyājaṃ sattvaṃ divyamavasthitamaho batāścaryamidaṃ nāsāyā me viniḥsṛtam
translation: “Is it the transcendental Being appearing in the form of a boar? What a miracle that it should come out of my nose!

devanagari: दृष्टोऽङ्गुष्ठशिरोमात्रः क्षणाद्‍गण्डशिलासमः । अपि स्विद्‍भगवानेष यज्ञो मे खेदयन्मनः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭo'ṅguṣṭhaśiromātraḥ kṣaṇād‍gaṇḍaśilāsamaḥapi svid‍bhagavāneṣa yajño me khedayanmanaḥ
translation: It appeared like the tip of a thumb and in a moment it became as big as a great boulder. Can this be the Divine Sacrifice (i.e. Lord Viṣṇu) himself who is trying my mind to exhaustion (by concealing his real form)?”

devanagari: इति मीमांसतस्तस्य ब्रह्मणः सह सूनुभिः । भगवान् यज्ञपुरुषो जगर्जागेन्द्रसन्निभः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti mīmāṃsatastasya brahmaṇaḥ saha sūnubhiḥbhagavān yajñapuruṣo jagarjāgendrasannibhaḥ
translation: While Brahmā was deeply thinking over it along with his sons, the glorious Supreme Sacrifice-Man (Yajña-Puruṣa) who was like a mountain thundered forth.

devanagari: ब्रह्माणं हर्षयामास हरिस्तांश्च द्विजोत्तमान् । स्वगर्जितेन ककुभः प्रतिस्वनयता विभुः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmāṇaṃ harṣayāmāsa haristāṃśca dvijottamānsvagarjitena kakubhaḥ pratisvanayatā vibhuḥ
translation: The all-pervading Hari thrilled with rapture, god Brahmā and the excellent Brahmins, by his roar which reverberated the directions.

devanagari: निशम्य ते घर्घरितं स्वखेद । क्षयिष्णु मायामयसूकरस्य । जनस्तपःसत्यनिवासिनस्ते त्रिभिः । पवित्रैर्मुनयोऽगृणन् स्म ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: niśamya te ghargharitaṃ svakhedakṣayiṣṇu māyāmayasūkarasyajanastapaḥsatyanivāsinaste tribhiḥpavitrairmunayo'gṛṇan sma
translation: Having heard the grief-removing roar of the (Being who had assumed the) Boar form by his Māyā, the sages who were residents of the same Tapas and Satya Iokas (worlds) praised him with the holy trinity (of the Vedas).

devanagari: तेषां सतां वेदवितानमूर्तिः । ब्रह्मावधार्यात्मगुणानुवादम् । विनद्य भूयो विबुधोदयाय । गजेन्द्रलीलो जलमाविवेश ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ satāṃ vedavitānamūrtiḥbrahmāvadhāryātmaguṇānuvādamvinadya bhūyo vibudhodayāyagajendralīlo jalamāviveśa
translation: Knowing that it is the Veda extolling his attributes that is chanted by the great (god Brahmā and the sages), he whose personality is described in the Vedas[5] again roared and for the prosperity of gods, rushed into the water like a big sporting elephant.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Veda-vitāna-mūrti [mūrtiḥ]— (i) One whose body is sacrifice (Bhāgavata Candrikā)
(ii) Whose form was entire Veda itself (VC.)

devanagari: उत्क्षिप्तवालः खचरः कठोरः । सटा विधुन्वन् खररोमशत्वक् । खुराहताभ्रः सितदंष्ट्र ईक्षा । ज्योतिर्बभासे भगवान्महीध्रः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: utkṣiptavālaḥ khacaraḥ kaṭhoraḥsaṭā vidhunvan khararomaśatvakkhurāhatābhraḥ sitadaṃṣṭra īkṣājyotirbabhāse bhagavānmahīdhraḥ
translation: He moved through the sky with his tail held aloft. With his shaking mane and kicking hoofs he dispersed the clouds. His boḍy was hard with tough hide bristled with sharp, erect hair. The Lord, the saviour of the earth, appeared brilliant with his white tusk and shining eyes.

devanagari: घ्राणेन पृथ्व्याः पदवीं विजिघ्रन् । क्रोडापदेशः स्वयमध्वराङ्गः । करालदंष्ट्रोऽप्यकरालदृग्भ्याम् । उद्वीक्ष्य विप्रान् गृणतोऽविशत्कम् ॥ ...
english_devnagari: ghrāṇena pṛthvyāḥ padavīṃ vijighrankroḍāpadeśaḥ svayamadhvarāṅgaḥkarāladaṃṣṭro'pyakarāladṛgbhyāmudvīkṣya viprān gṛṇato'viśatkam
translation: Though he was sacrifice itself in person, He assumed the form of a boar and was scenting the track of the earth. Though his tusks were sharp, he gently looked at the Brāhmaṇas who were praising him and dashed into water.

devanagari: स वज्रकूटाङ्गनिपातवेग । विशीर्णकुक्षिः स्तनयन्नुदन्वान् । उत्सृष्टदीर्घोर्मिभुजैरिवार्तः । चुक्रोश यज्ञेश्वर पाहि मेति ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vajrakūṭāṅganipātavegaviśīrṇakukṣiḥ stanayannudanvānutsṛṣṭadīrghormibhujairivārtaḥcukrośa yajñeśvara pāhi meti
translation: The roaring ocean whose side was shattered by the velocity of the falling of his body which was like a mountain of adamantine peaks, spread out his arms in the form of swelling surges and as if out of affliction, yelled out, ‘Oh Lord of Sacrifices, protect me’.

devanagari: खुरैः क्षुरप्रैर्दरयंस्तदाप । उत्पारपारं त्रिपरू रसायाम् । ददर्श गां तत्र सुषुप्सुरग्रे । यां जीवधानीं स्वयमभ्यधत्त ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: khuraiḥ kṣuraprairdarayaṃstadāpautpārapāraṃ triparū rasāyāmdadarśa gāṃ tatra suṣupsuragreyāṃ jīvadhānīṃ svayamabhyadhatta
translation: Then cutting down the unfathomable waters to the bottom with his sharp arrow-like hoofs, the Lord who was sacrifice incarnate (lit. who had three joints in the form of savanas or sacrificial acts), saw the earth in rasātala where at the time of Deluge (Pralaya) he desired to sleep, placing the earth along with the beings, in his stomach.

devanagari: स्वदंष्ट्रयोद्धृत्य महीं निमग्नां । स उत्थितः संरुरुचे रसायाः । तत्रापि दैत्यं गदयापतन्तं । सुनाभसन्दीपिततीव्रमन्युः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: svadaṃṣṭrayoddhṛtya mahīṃ nimagnāṃsa utthitaḥ saṃruruce rasāyāḥtatrāpi daityaṃ gadayāpatantaṃsunābhasandīpitatīvramanyuḥ
translation: He appeared extremely resplendent when he rose up pulling out by his tusks the submerged earth from the rasātala. Even there (in the sea), he whose intensified rage was like the Sudarśana Cakra[6], saw a demon (Daitya) of irresistible prowess attacking him with a mace and (thus) obstructing him. He killed him easily as a lion kills an elephant. With his cheeks red with the (mudlike) clotted blood of the demon, he appeared like a big elephant who had turned up (red chalk from) the earth.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
‘Whose rage was intensified by Sudarśana’—Subodhinī
Subodhinī explains: When the mace and weapons of Viṣṇu proved ineffective to kill the demon, the Suḍarśana disc had to remind Viṣṇu of the misdeeds of the demon and intensified his anger to finish with the demon.

devanagari: जघान रुन्धानमसह्यविक्रमं । स लीलयेभं मृगराडिवाम्भसि । तद्रक्तपङ्काङ्कितगण्डतुण्डो । यथा गजेन्द्रो जगतीं विभिन्दन् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: jaghāna rundhānamasahyavikramaṃsa līlayebhaṃ mṛgarāḍivāmbhasitadraktapaṅkāṅkitagaṇḍatuṇḍoyathā gajendro jagatīṃ vibhindan
translation: He appeared extremely resplendent when he rose up pulling out by his tusks the submerged earth from the rasātala. Even there (in the sea), he whose intensified rage was like the Sudarśana Cakra[6], saw a demon (Daitya) of irresistible prowess attacking him with a mace and (thus) obstructing him. He killed him easily as a lion kills an elephant. With his cheeks red with the (mudlike) clotted blood of the demon, he appeared like a big elephant who had turned up (red chalk from) the earth.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
‘Whose rage was intensified by Sudarśana’—Subodhinī
Subodhinī explains: When the mace and weapons of Viṣṇu proved ineffective to kill the demon, the Suḍarśana disc had to remind Viṣṇu of the misdeeds of the demon and intensified his anger to finish with the demon.

devanagari: तमालनीलं सितदन्तकोट्या । क्ष्मामुत्क्षिपन्तं गजलीलयाङ्ग । प्रज्ञाय बद्धाञ्जलयोऽनुवाकैः । विरिञ्चिमुख्या उपतस्थुरीशम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tamālanīlaṃ sitadantakoṭyākṣmāmutkṣipantaṃ gajalīlayāṅgaprajñāya baddhāñjalayo'nuvākaiḥviriñcimukhyā upatasthurīśam
translation: [7] Oh Vidura, (sages and gods) of whom Brahmā was the leader recognised the Lord (Varāha) of tamāla-like blue complexion, who was sportively lifting up the earth by the tip of his tusks like an elephant. They folded their hands and prayed him with words like Vedic sūktas.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
VC. and Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa quoting Bhāgavatāmṛta kārikā maintain that Maitreya has mixed up two Boar incarnations—the white one in Svāyambhuva Age and the blue one in the Cākṣuṣa Manvantara.

devanagari: ऋषय ऊचुः । जितं जितं तेऽजित यज्ञभावन । त्रयीं तनुं स्वां परिधुन्वते नमः । यद् रोमगर्तेषु निलिल्युरध्वराः । तस्मै नमः कारणसूकराय ते ॥ ३४...
english_devnagari: ṛṣaya ūcuḥjitaṃ jitaṃ te'jita yajñabhāvanatrayīṃ tanuṃ svāṃ paridhunvate namaḥyad romagarteṣu nililyuradhvarāḥtasmai namaḥ kāraṇasūkarāya te
translation:
Sages said:
“Victory to you, Oh unconquerable Lord! Oh creator of sacrifices![8] We bow to you who are shaking your body composed of three Vedas. We salute to you who have assumed this boar form (to lift up the earth), in the pores of whose hair sacrifices lie hidden.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
yajña-bhāvana: (i) Who is invoked by sacrifices—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā (ii) The creator or cause of all sacriñces—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa, Bhāgavata Candrikā,VC. Subodhinī (iii) Who manifests himself by sacrifices—Dīpinī (iv) Who is worshipped as Yajña with its different parts—Bhāgavata Candrikā (v) Who is propitiated by sacriñces—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: रूपं तवैतन्ननु दुष्कृतात्मनां । दुर्दर्शनं देव यदध्वरात्मकम् । छन्दांसि यस्य त्वचि बर्हिरोम । स्वाज्यं दृशि त्वङ्घ्रिषु चातुर्होत्रम् ॥...
english_devnagari: rūpaṃ tavaitannanu duṣkṛtātmanāṃdurdarśanaṃ deva yadadhvarātmakamchandāṃsi yasya tvaci barhiromasvājyaṃ dṛśi tvaṅghriṣu cāturhotram
translation: [9] Oh Lord, the sacrificial form of your body is really very difficult to look at by the sinful people. The Vedic metres are found in your skin, the kuśa grass in your hair, the sacred clarified butter in your eyes, and the fourfold duties of the sacrificial priests in your feet.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
The conception of Yajña-Varāha was formulated first in Va P. 6.16.23. It was adopted later by MP. 248.67-73, Viṣṇu Purāṇa 1.4.32-33, BRP. 213.33.7. PP. Sṛṣṭi-kāṇḍa 16.55.61, Harivaṃśa P. 1.41.29-33. The concept was so popular that in addition to literary reference in Viṣṇu Smṛti I.3-9, Viṣṇudharmottara 1.2.3-8 we have Yajña-Varāha images (e.g. that at Vihāra in North Gujarat). The two aspects of this concept viz. (i) its significance as the symbol of Vedic cosmogony and (ii) the correspondence between the elements of sacrifices and the parts ofVarāha’s body are discussed by V.S. Agrawala MP—A Study, pp.313ff.

devanagari: स्रुक्तुण्ड आसीत्स्रुव ईश नासयोः । इडोदरे चमसाः कर्णरन्ध्रे । प्राशित्रमास्ये ग्रसने ग्रहास्तु ते । यच्चर्वणं ते भगवन्नग्निहोत्रम् ॥ ३६...
english_devnagari: sruktuṇḍa āsītsruva īśa nāsayoḥiḍodare camasāḥ karṇarandhreprāśitramāsye grasane grahāstu teyaccarvaṇaṃ te bhagavannagnihotram
translation: Oh Lord, sruk is in your mouth, sruvas are in your nostrils, Iḍā in your stomach, and camasas in your ears; prāśitra (the vessel for Brahmā’s share) in your mouth, grahas (a kind of cup for soma) in your throat. Agnihotra in your chewing.

devanagari: दीक्षानुजन्मोपसदः शिरोधरं । त्वं प्रायणीयोदयनीयदंष्ट्रः । जिह्वा प्रवर्ग्यस्तव शीर्षकं क्रतोः । सभ्यावसथ्यं चितयोऽसवो हि ते ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: dīkṣānujanmopasadaḥ śirodharaṃtvaṃ prāyaṇīyodayanīyadaṃṣṭraḥjihvā pravargyastava śīrṣakaṃ kratoḥsabhyāvasathyaṃ citayo'savo hi te
translation: Your frequency of incarnation is the iṣṭi (sacrificial act) called dīkṣā. The three iṣṭis called upasads form your neck. Prāyaṇīya (iṣṭi after dīkṣā) and udayanīya (iṣṭi after completion of sacrifice) are your two tusks. The Pravargya Mahāvīra[10] ceremony preliminary to Soma sacrifice) is your tongue. The two fires sabhya and āvasathya form your head. You are the sacrifice. The arrangement of bricks for sacrifice is your vital breath.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Apte’s PSD 668. Mahāvīra is a sacrificial vessel. When it is red hot, ghee and milk is to be put in it(Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa). This is performed before every upasad (Bhāvāratha Dīpikā).

devanagari: सोमस्तु रेतः सवनान्यवस्थितिः । संस्थाविभेदास्तव देव धातवः । सत्राणि सर्वाणि शरीरसन्धि । स्त्वं सर्वयज्ञक्रतुरिष्टिबन्धनः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: somastu retaḥ savanānyavasthitiḥsaṃsthāvibhedāstava deva dhātavaḥsatrāṇi sarvāṇi śarīrasandhistvaṃ sarvayajñakraturiṣṭibandhanaḥ
translation: Your semen is the soma juice. The three savanas (morning, noon and evening) form your seat. The seven sacrifices (Agniṣṭoma, Atyagniṣṭoma, Ukthya, Ṣoḍaṣī, Vājapeya, Atirātra and Āptoryāma) are your dhātus (constituents of the body). All kinds of satras (sacrificial sessions) are the joints of your body. You are both kratus (sacrifices in which soma juice was extracted) and yajñas (sacrifices without the extraction of soma juice). Iṣṭis in sacrifices are your tendons.

devanagari: नमो नमस्तेऽखिलमन्त्रदेवता । द्रव्याय सर्वक्रतवे क्रियात्मने । वैराग्यभक्त्यात्मजयानुभावित । ज्ञानाय विद्यागुरवे नमो नमः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: namo namaste'khilamantradevatādravyāya sarvakratave kriyātmanevairāgyabhaktyātmajayānubhāvitajñānāya vidyāgurave namo namaḥ
translation: We bow again and again to you who are all the mantras, deities, sacrificial materials, sacrificial acts and the performance of all acts. We repeatedly salute to you who are knowledge realised by nonattachment, devotion and self-control, and who are the preceptor of that knowledge.

devanagari: दंष्ट्राग्रकोट्या भगवंस्त्वया धृता । विराजते भूधर भूः सभूधरा । यथा वनान्निःसरतो दता धृता । मतङ्गजेन्द्रस्य सपत्रपद्मिनी ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: daṃṣṭrāgrakoṭyā bhagavaṃstvayā dhṛtāvirājate bhūdhara bhūḥ sabhūdharāyathā vanānniḥsarato datā dhṛtāmataṅgajendrasya sapatrapadminī
translation: Oh Supreme Lord, the support of the earth, the earth along with the mountains which you have borne upon the tip of your tusk, appears beautiful like a lotus plant with leaves resting on the tusk of a big elephant.

devanagari: त्रयीमयं रूपमिदं च सौकरं । भूमण्डलेनाथ दता धृतेन ते । चकास्ति शृङ्गोढघनेन भूयसा । कुलाचलेन्द्रस्य यथैव विभ्रमः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: trayīmayaṃ rūpamidaṃ ca saukaraṃbhūmaṇḍalenātha datā dhṛtena tecakāsti śṛṅgoḍhaghanena bhūyasākulācalendrasya yathaiva vibhramaḥ
translation: This your boar form consisting of three Vedas, with the sphere of the earth supported by your tusk, appears shining like a great enchantingly beautiful Kula Parvata[11] with a cloud resting on its peak.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Kulācala: A class of seven mountains which are supposed to exist in each division of the continent. Their names are: Mahendra, Malaya, Sahya, Śuktimān, Ṛkṣaparvata, Vindhya and Pāriyātra.

devanagari: संस्थापयैनां जगतां सतस्थुषां । लोकाय पत्‍नीमसि मातरं पिता । विधेम चास्यै नमसा सह त्वया । यस्यां स्वतेजोऽग्निमिवारणावधाः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃsthāpayaināṃ jagatāṃ satasthuṣāṃlokāya pat‍nīmasi mātaraṃ pitāvidhema cāsyai namasā saha tvayāyasyāṃ svatejo'gnimivāraṇāvadhāḥ
translation: Establish firmly your consort, this earth, the mother of the world of the movables and immovables, for the residence of the people; for you are the father or the Protector. We offer our salutations to her along with you. You have deposited your vital power in her as the Fire is dormant in araṇi—(a piece of Śami wood used for creating sacrificial fire by friction with another piece).

devanagari: कः श्रद्दधीतान्यतमस्तव प्रभो । रसां गताया भुव उद्विबर्हणम् । न विस्मयोऽसौ त्वयि विश्वविस्मये । यो माययेदं ससृजेऽतिविस्मयम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: kaḥ śraddadhītānyatamastava prabhorasāṃ gatāyā bhuva udvibarhaṇamna vismayo'sau tvayi viśvavismayeyo māyayedaṃ sasṛje'tivismayam
translation: Oh Supreme ruler, who else can aspire or determine to lift up the earth which sank down to the nethermost region (rasātala)? In your case, this is not a miracle as you have created this extremely wonderful world by your Māyā power.

devanagari: विधुन्वता वेदमयं निजं वपुः । जनस्तपःसत्यनिवासिनो वयम् । सटाशिखोद्धूत शिवाम्बुबिन्दुभिः । विमृज्यमाना भृशमीश पाविताः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: vidhunvatā vedamayaṃ nijaṃ vapuḥjanastapaḥsatyanivāsino vayamsaṭāśikhoddhūta śivāmbubindubhiḥvimṛjyamānā bhṛśamīśa pāvitāḥ
translation: Oh Almighty, we, the residents of the Jana, Tapas and Satya regions have been thoroughly purified by the sanctifying drops of water sprayed from the ends of your bristle like hair while you shake your body which is composed of the Vedas.

devanagari: स वै बत भ्रष्टमतिस्तवैष ते । यः कर्मणां पारमपारकर्मणः । यद्योगमायागुणयोगमोहितं । विश्वं समस्तं भगवन्विधेहि शम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai bata bhraṣṭamatistavaiṣa teyaḥ karmaṇāṃ pāramapārakarmaṇaḥyadyogamāyāguṇayogamohitaṃviśvaṃ samastaṃ bhagavanvidhehi śam
translation: He who aspires to reach to the end of (i.e. to know thoroughly) all your deeds which are endless, must really have lost his senses. The whole universe is deluded by the association of the attributes of your yoga māyā. Oh Supreme Lord, bless (us) with happiness.”

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इत्युपस्थीयमानस्तैः मुनिभिर्ब्रह्मवादिभिः । सलिले स्वखुराक्रान्त उपाधत्तावितावनिम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaityupasthīyamānastaiḥ munibhirbrahmavādibhiḥsalile svakhurākrānta upādhattāvitāvanim
translation:
Maitreya said:
While the Protector (Varāha) was thus praised by the sages, the knowers of Brahman, he stabilised the waters by his hoofs and placed the earth on them.

devanagari: स इत्थं भगवानुर्वीं विष्वक्सेनः प्रजापतिः । रसाया लीलयोन्नीतां अप्सु न्यस्य ययौ हरिः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa itthaṃ bhagavānurvīṃ viṣvaksenaḥ prajāpatiḥrasāyā līlayonnītāṃ apsu nyasya yayau hariḥ
translation: The Supreme Lord Hari, the Viṣvaksena and the protector of the world, placed on waters the earth which he easily lifted up from rasātala and disappeared (lit. departed).

devanagari: य एवमेतां हरिमेधसो हरेः । कथां सुभद्रां कथनीयमायिनः । शृण्वीत भक्त्या श्रवयेत वोशतीं । जनार्दनोऽस्याशु हृदि प्रसीदति ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: ya evametāṃ harimedhaso hareḥkathāṃ subhadrāṃ kathanīyamāyinaḥśṛṇvīta bhaktyā śravayeta vośatīṃjanārdano'syāśu hṛdi prasīdati
translation: Janārdana (Hari) will be immediately pleased in his heart on him who will thus devotedly hear or make others hear this extremely auspicious and enchanting tale of Hari whose miraculous deeds are worth describing and knowledge about whom destroys all miseries.

devanagari: तस्मिन्प्रसन्ने सकलाशिषां प्रभौ । किं दुर्लभं ताभिरलं लवात्मभिः । अनन्यदृष्ट्या भजतां गुहाशयः । स्वयं विधत्ते स्वगतिं परः पराम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminprasanne sakalāśiṣāṃ prabhaukiṃ durlabhaṃ tābhiralaṃ lavātmabhiḥananyadṛṣṭyā bhajatāṃ guhāśayaḥsvayaṃ vidhatte svagatiṃ paraḥ parām
translation: What is there difficult to be achieved when the Lord of all blessings is pleased? Enough with those (pleasures) of insignificant value. The Supreme Lord, the Dweller in the hearts of all, of his own accord, confers summum bonum on those who are devoted to him without any ulterior motive.

devanagari: को नाम लोके पुरुषार्थसारवित् । पुराकथानां भगवत्कथासुधाम् । आपीय कर्णाञ्जलिभिर्भवापहा । महो विरज्येत विना नरेतरम् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: ko nāma loke puruṣārthasāravitpurākathānāṃ bhagavatkathāsudhāmāpīya karṇāñjalibhirbhavāpahāmaho virajyeta vinā naretaram
translation:
Out of the stories of yore, the nectar-like narratives of the Lord put an end to Saṃsāra. Having drunk these through the ears (comparable to the cavity of folded hands) what person who has understood the essential purpose of life, can get satiated (with them). (If such there be), he must be other than a human being.
[back to top]
